text:
Top center title: **Announcements**

Top right small toolbar icons/text:
- blue circular icon
- **Move**
- **Draw**
- **Clear**
- **Hide controls**
- red **X**

Main numbered list:

**1.** **First midterm is next Monday, Feb 23rd**  
  **-- we will provide the bubble sheet for the MC part**

**2.** **DSP for first midterm:  GPB 100, 5 pm.**

**3.** **Review Session:  Q&A, Thurs, Feb 19th 5 pm**  
  **Place: Physics 4**

**4.** **Prepare**  
  **Lecture:** Anything said in class to be fair game for the exam.  
  **Textbook:** Responsible for section headings and key terms.  
    You should understand the figures.  
  **Reader articles:** Major conceptual themes, methods, key results

Layout:
- White slide background, black serif text
- Numbered items aligned left
- Item 4 has indented subpoints for Lecture, Textbook, Reader articles
- “You should understand the figures.” is further indented under Textbook.

Title: “Anatomical Measures from” (title cut off at end)

Subheading: “Structural changes in neurological disease/disorders”

Bulleted/indented list:
- “Vascular Disorders (e.g., stroke, cerebral vascular accident)”
- “Tumors”
- “Degenerative disorders/atrophy (cell death)”
- “Traumatic brain injury (TBI)” — blue, underlined
  - indented sub-point: “Most common form of neurological injury in young adults”

Top-right overlay toolbar with small icons; one blue circular icon at left of toolbar, followed by several small gray icons, and a red “X” icon at far right.

Title: “Anatomical Measures from” (title cut off at end)

Subheading: “Structural changes in neurological disease/disorders”

Bulleted/line list:
- “Vascular Disorders (e.g., stroke, cerebral vascular accident)”
- “Tumors”
- “Degenerative disorders/atrophy (cell death)”
- “Traumatic brain injury (TBI)” — blue text

Indented under “Traumatic brain injury (TBI)”:
- “Most common form of neurological injury in young adults”
- “Chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CPE)”

Bottom row of images:
- Left: black-background MRI panel with two brain scans labeled at top “A” and “B”
- Caption under left MRI: “60 year old retired pro”
- Middle: pathology/brain section panel; small red text at top appears “Ventricle enlarged”; lower-left small label: “Normal 27 year old”; lower-right small label: “Traumatic encephalopathy”
- Caption under middle/right images: “27 year old: Aaron Hernandez”
- Far right: small color photo of a football player in uniform, jersey number “81”

Top-right overlay from video player/annotation toolbar:
small icons in a row including a blue circular undo-style arrow, drawing/tool icons, and a red “X”.

Anatomical Measures from

Volume: grey, white, both

Left section:
- Small bullet point to the left of a group of brain images
- 8 colored cortical surface brain renderings arranged in two rows
- Top row: 2 larger lateral brain views, color scale over cortex
- Bottom row: 6 smaller brain views from different angles, multicolor statistical overlays
- Small labels under images too small to read clearly
- Caption below: “Cortical thickness and IQ”

Center-right upper section:
- Black rectangular panel with 3 subpanels
- Left subpanel: coronal/oblique grayscale brain MRI slices with colored highlighted regions
- Magenta labels and arrows: “right IFG”, “right STG”
- White letter near lower left: “C)”
- Right subpanel: scatter plot
  - y-axis label too small to read clearly
  - x-axis label: “Global Musical Score”
  - legend: “Amusics”, “Controls”
  - downward-sloping dotted regression line
- Caption below: “Temporal lobe thickness and amusia”

Lower-right section:
- Left: small grid of grayscale brain MRI slices with green highlighted regions
- Right: bar chart with grouped vertical bars
  - legend with colored boxes:
    - green: “Early Blind”
    - red: “Late Blind”
    - black text nearby: “Hippocampus size in blind individuals”
  - several yellow/orange/green/red bars across categories
  - axis text too small to read clearly

Top-right overlay from video/screen controls:
- Row of small icons with labels:
  - blue circular icon
  - pen icon: “Draw”
  - eraser icon: “Erase”
  - rectangle icon: “Shapes”
  - text icon: “Texts”
  - trash/X icon: “Close”

Title: “Anatomical Measures from”

Text:
- “Volume:  grey, white, both”
- “Tractography:  Diffusion tensor imaging (DTI)” — blue, underlined
- “Inside membrane: Water flow restricted by axon wall”
- “Outside membrane: Water flow is unrestricted, random”
- “DTI:  Track flow direction to determine white matter tracts”

Images under the DTI text:
- Left image: small brain scan with dark background, multicolored tract lines/fibers through the brain; label below: “horizontal”
- Right image: small coronal brain slice with grayscale anatomical background and colored tract overlays in blue, magenta, green, red; label below: “coronal”

Top-right overlay toolbar/icons visible:
- blue circular icon
- small gray icons and labels including “Mouse”, “Pen”, “Eraser”, “Spot”, “Cursor”, “Mute”, “Exit”

Anatomical Measures from

Volume: grey, white, both

Tractography: Diffusion tensor imaging (DTI)

Left image: grid of many axial brain slices; each slice overlaid with colored tract directions, mainly red, green, and blue. Caption:
DTI of professional boxers
(colored: lower tract integrity)

Right image: grayscale brain image with white-matter tracts visible; a purple rectangular outline over left-sided region. Caption:
Dyslexia
(abnormalities are LH)

Text to the right of the right image:
Cause?
Or effect?

Title: “Human Functional Neuroimaging”

Bullet/header: “Measuring metabolic correlates of neural activity”

Indented bullet: “Neural activity demands lots of oxygen”

Indented section: “Positron Emission Tomography (PET)”
- “Inject radioactive tracer such as ¹⁵O”
- “Measure location of decaying isotopes”

Indented section: “Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI)”
- “Accessible”
- “non-invasive (no radioactive tracer)”
- “lower cost”

Lower right photo: hospital imaging room with a cylindrical scanner; patient lying on a narrow bed entering the scanner head-first; one person in white clothing standing at right near the bed; two people at left side of scanner; tiled floor, ceiling lights, light-colored walls.

Title centered at top: “Physiology of fMRI”

Left text:
“Neural activity leads to”
1. “Initial drop in local oxygen”
2. “Increase in local blood flow”
“(delayed via astrocyte signaling)”
3. “Oversupply of local oxygen”

Next line:
“Oxygenated and deoxygenated blood have different magnetic properties.”

Bottom-left text:
“BOLD: Blood oxygenation level dependent response”
“Ratio of oxygenated to deoxygenated blood rises due to oversupply”
“Plotted as % signal change”

Upper-right cluster of diagrams:
- Small heading above left diagram: “Neurovascular coupling”
- Small heading above middle/right diagram: “Hemodynamic response”
- Red text near top-right: “the BOLD MRI response”

Neurovascular coupling diagram details:
- Stylized neuron/astrocyte/capillary schematic
- Purple star-like cell body at upper left connected by process toward vessel
- Light blue branching astrocyte-like cell spanning center
- Red blood vessel/capillary along bottom, with yellow/orange glow around part of it
- Small labels near vessel/cell structures, including “Ca2+” and “glutamate”

Hemodynamic response diagram details:
- Circular vessel cross-section in center with many small red circles inside
- Black arrow from left schematic to this vessel cross-section
- Red plus sign above vessel cross-section
- Purple star-like cell at right
- Label above/near top: “↑ blood supply”
- Label near vessel: “↑ blood flow”
- Arrow from vessel/cell schematic toward graph at far right

Far-right graph:
- Axes with vertical axis labeled “Signal”
- Horizontal axis labeled “Time”
- Magenta/purple response curve: starts near baseline, rises steeply to a peak, then falls below baseline slightly, then returns toward baseline
- Small tick labels under x-axis; small y-axis markings present but too small to read clearly

Title: **Physiology of fMRI**

Left text:
- **Neural activity leads to**
  1. **Initial drop in local oxygen**
  2. **Increase in local blood flow**  
     **(delayed via astrocyte signaling)**
  3. **Oversupply of local oxygen**

- **Oxygenated and deoxygenated blood have different magnetic properties.**

- **BOLD: Blood oxygenation level dependent response**  
  **Ratio of oxygenated to deoxygenated blood rises due to oversupply**  
  **Plotted as % signal change**

- Blue text:  
  **Alternative measure: Beta weight**  
  **Contribution of independent variable to BOLD change**

Right-side small diagrams:
- Row of four small labels/images across top: **Neurovascular coupling**, **Hemodynamic response**, **the BOLD MRI response**
- Left small schematic: neuron/astrocyte/blood vessel interaction; purple neuron-like cells, cyan astrocyte-like branching cell, red/yellow vessel-like structure; arrows indicating signaling between neural tissue and vessel
- Middle small schematic: circular red blood-cell cluster inside vessel-like boundary with small purple star-shaped cells nearby; plus/minus symbols and a small arrowed timeline-like sequence above
- Right small graph: magenta hemodynamic-response curve on axes; rises to a peak then falls; horizontal axis labeled **Time (s)**; vertical axis indicates signal/BOLD response; small numeric ticks not legible

Layout:
- Main lecture text on left/center
- Illustrations grouped in upper-right
- Plain light background

Title centered at top: “Physiology of fMRI”

Top right toolbar icons/text:
blue circular back arrow icon; small icons with labels “Pen”, “Highlighter”, “Eraser”, “Shapes”, “Text”, red “X”

Left brain image:
grayscale MRI slice, rectangular, black background
top label above image: “top of head”
left side vertical label: “face”
right side vertical label: “back of head”
below image text:
“Voxels: 3-d pixels
(spatial resolution)”

Right brain image:
larger grayscale MRI slice, black background
top label above image: “top of head”
left side vertical label: “face”
right side vertical label: “back of head”

Red annotation on right image:
red arrow pointing to a small region in the upper-right area of the bright brain tissue
red text near arrow tip: “Voxel (~ 15 mm³)”

Inset plot over lower middle of right image:
title in gray box: “BOLD activity in one voxel”
small graph with y-axis tick labels “0.8”, “0.6”, “0.4”, “0.2”, “0.0”
x-axis tick labels from left to right: “0”, “50”, “100”, “150”, “200”
light blue fluctuating time-series line across the plot

Below right image text:
“BOLD in 1 voxel
(temporal resolution)”

Experimental Methods

Block design

Slow alternation between stimulus conditions (40 s)

- Fixation Only
- Static dot displays
- Moving dot displays

BOLD signal takes 6-10 s to peak.

Plot data as difference in percent signal change from control condition.

Right side: two small time-course plots stacked vertically.

Top plot title: “Primary visual (V1)”
- y-axis label: “% Increase from fixation”
- x-axis labeled in repeating blocks: “Fixation”, “Static”, “Moving”, “Fixation”, “Static”, “Moving”
- red jagged response trace over alternating shaded stimulus blocks
- yellow vertical markers/brackets near transitions/peaks

Bottom plot title: “Higher-order visual (V5)”
- y-axis label: “% Increase from fixation”
- x-axis labeled in repeating blocks: “Fixation”, “Static”, “Moving”, “Fixation”, “Static”, “Moving”
- red jagged response trace over alternating shaded stimulus blocks
- yellow vertical markers/brackets near transitions/peaks

Bottom-right bar chart title: “Change in BOLD”
- y-axis: “% Increase from Fixation”, ticks 0, 1, 2, 3
- x-axis categories: “Static”, “Moving”
- legend: V1 (blue), V5 (green)
- bars:
  - Static: V1 ≈ 2.5, V5 ≈ 1
  - Moving: V1 ≈ 2, V5 ≈ 2.8–3

Overall content relation: V1 responds to both static and moving dot displays; V5 responds much more strongly to moving than static displays.

Title: **Experimental Methods**

Text:
- **Block design**
- **Event-related design**
  - Align scans with respect to specific events.
  - Temporal sensitivity is slower than events but use algorithms to estimate BOLD changes to specific events
  - Can interleave experimental conditions

Small graph under the bullets:
- Legend at top right of graph:
  - red line: **Stimulus presentations**
  - blue line: **BOLD responses**
- Left side label near graph: **Event-related**
- y-axis origin label: **0**
- x-axis label: **Time**
- x-axis tick labels: **0, 16, 32, 48, 64, 80**
- Several thin red vertical bars at irregular times along the baseline, representing stimulus presentations
- Blue waveform above baseline with multiple peaks following the red bars, representing BOLD responses

Top center overlay from video player/meeting controls:
- blue circular icon
- icons with labels: **Mute**, **Stop Video**, **Security**, **Participants**, **Chat**, **Share Screen**, **Record**, **Reactions**, **Apps**

Main graph:
- y-axis label: “Signal change”
- x-axis label: “Time (s)”
- x-axis tick marks: 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14
- y-axis tick marks: -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
- Two line plots:
  - red line labeled “Remembered”
  - blue line labeled “Forgotten”
- Both start near 0 at time 0
- Forgotten: small rise around 2–4 s to about 1, then declines toward 0 by 8–10 s, slightly below 0 by 12–14 s
- Remembered: steep rise to peak near 4 at about 4 s, then declines to about 2 at 6 s, near 1 at 8 s, near 0.2 at 10 s, around 0 at 12–14 s

Title: “BOLD time-locked to stimulus onset during study phase”

Subheading: “Remembered vs. Forgotten”

Text below: “Effect only observed in a few regions”

Left side:
- Two brain-image panels with labels beneath:
  - “a Posterior LIFG”
  - “b Parahippocampus/fusiform gml”
- Each panel consists of small grayscale brain slices with colored activation overlays/arrows marking regions.

Right side: four line graphs, arranged 2×2. All have y-axis label “Signal change (%)” and x-axis label “Time (s)”. X-axis ticks run from 0 to 14 in steps of 2. Two lines per graph: red = “Remembered”, blue = “Forgotten”, with those words written near the corresponding curves in the left-column graphs.

Top left graph title: “Left Frontal”
- Red remembered curve: starts near 0 at 0 s, rises to peak around 4 s near 4%, then declines gradually toward ~0 by 10–12 s, slightly below 0 by 14 s.
- Blue forgotten curve: small rise to about 1–1.5% around 4 s, then falls to ~0 by 8–10 s, slightly below 0 after.
- Error bars shown on points.

Top right graph title: “Primary L. Visual”
- Red and blue curves nearly overlap.
- Both rise steeply from 0 to peak around 4–5% at 4 s, then decline to near 0 by 8–10 s, slightly below 0 by 12–14 s.
- Error bars shown.

Bottom left graph title: “Left Temporal”
- Red remembered curve: rises from 0 to peak around 5% at 4 s, then drops steeply below 0 around 8–10 s, slight recovery by 14 s.
- Blue forgotten curve: rises to lower peak around 4% at 4 s, then similarly declines below 0 and slightly recovers.
- Error bars shown.

Bottom right graph title: “Primary R Motor”
- Red and blue curves nearly overlap.
- Both peak around 4–5% at 4 s, then decline to near 0 by 8–10 s, slightly below 0 around 10–12 s, small increase by 14 s.
- Error bars shown.
